rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes for port-level contracts, and in implementation scopes for bindings after subcomponents and connections.
Use visible feature names and concrete bounds when defining range assumptions and guarantees in component type contracts.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE annex blocks are placed in valid AADL scopes to maintain architectural integrity.
Define value range constraints using boolean expressions that reference visible identifiers and legal feature names.
Align helper declaration types with actual expression types to avoid type mismatches in AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `eq variable : bool = condition;` to define range checks in AGREE contracts.
Place annex agree blocks within component type or implementation scopes as appropriate.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes or using subcomponent ports in type contracts without proper variable representation.
Do not define range assumptions or guarantees without visible identifiers or concrete bounds.
